title: harness-boot spec.yaml
description: v2.3.8 — promotes the eight metadata.* blocks (formerly the v2.3.7 metadata.extensions.* experimental namespace) to native fields. Re-converted across the meta-domain samples; rationale: design/rfcs/v2.3.8-metadata-extensions-promotion.md
$comment: schema_version note: v2.3.8 is additive — every v2.3.7 spec still loads. metadata.extensions.* is accepted for backward compatibility but deprecated (slated for removal in v2.5.0).

version.description: Spec schema version (semver). The v2.3.8 loader accepts >=2.3.0.

mode.description: v0.9.6 — quality / ceremony tier switch. `prototype` lightens kickoff (1 bullet per agent), retro (machine sections only), design-review (skips autowire), and the Iron Law (1 declared evidence). `product` (the default when omitted) keeps the full strict path.

language.description: F-040 (v0.10.14) — preferred language for user-facing plugin output (work activate / dashboard / error messages). 'en' is the default and protects English adopters; 'ko' targets Korean users; 'auto' falls through to the system locale. Overridden by the HARNESS_LANG env var. Backend (code, schema, commit messages) stays English regardless.

brief.description: v0.5 additive — anchor for researcher / product-planner output. The normalized snapshot of brief.md derived from a one-line idea.

ui_surface.description: v0.5 additive — UI surface declaration. The orchestrator uses this as the routing key for the ux-architect → visual-designer (+audio-designer) → a11y-auditor → frontend-engineer chain.

items.description: Open string: web / mobile-ios / mobile-android / desktop / game / tui / etc.

performance_budget.description: v0.6 additive — per-feature performance budget. Used as the routing key for performance-engineer.

supersedes.description: v0.10 additive — the ids of earlier features this one replaces. The replaced features are encouraged to transition to status=archived in state.yaml. Self-reference and cycles are forbidden (validated by check.py Anchor drift).

superseded_by.description: v0.10 additive — the id of the feature that replaced this one (when present). `work.py --archive --superseded-by` fills it in as the inverse of supersedes.

integration_anchor.description: v0.10.1 additive — anchor declaring that, while this feature is alive (status=done), the import / reference of its modules[].source must be visible from these entry-point files. check.py's AnchorIntegration drift validates it. Catches the integration gap that a per-feature smoke alone misses (e.g. every feature's gates pass, but the main entry point is empty so end-to-end doesn't actually run). Paths are relative to project_root.

area.description: v0.10.6 additive — domain / area key the feature belongs to (e.g. 'auth', 'billing', 'gate', 'observability'). Will be used to group spec/features/<area>/F-N.yaml when sharding becomes necessary. For now it is documentation-only — users can fill it freely, no enum is enforced.

archived_at.description: v0.10.6 additive (declarative; clarified in v0.10.7) — the timestamp at which the feature was moved from the active spec into archive (ISO8601 UTC, e.g. '2026-04-27T00:00:00Z'). **Declarative — the user fills this into spec.yaml; work.py does not auto-fill it.** Runtime status is owned by state.yaml's status=archived (which work.py --archive does update). This field is for the future ~300-feature threshold (when entries move to spec/archive/YYYY.yaml) and for declaring archive intent at the spec level. When unset, the feature is active.

archive_reason.description: v0.10.6 additive (declarative; clarified in v0.10.7) — archive reason (1-2 lines). Filled by the user into spec.yaml alongside archived_at. The --reason option of work.py --archive is recorded only into events.log; it does not touch spec.yaml (that is the user's commit). Examples: 'feature complete · 6 mo unreferenced', 'replaced by external library', 'scope removed'.

digest.description: v0.10.6 additive — a 1-2 line LLM-context summary of the feature. When spec.yaml grows too large for a wholesale @import, the summary index (produced by scripts/spec/summary.py) extracts only this field. Falls back to `name` when unset.

area_scan.description: F-037 additive — per-feature override knob for fog-clear. Allows freezing chapters or pinning the resolved area set for that feature.

include_path.description: v0.10.6 additive — entry point for sharding. When set, this feature's detail is loaded from an external file (e.g. 'spec/features/auth/F-042.yaml'). Currently ignorable (forward-compat); activates around the ~1000-feature threshold.

prefixItems.description: Walking Skeleton (v2.3 Runtime-Verified First) — features[0] must have type='skeleton'. The first feature has to carry an end-to-end execution skeleton that passes through gate_5; subsequent features ride on top of it.

constraints.description: v0.6 structuring — definitions for tech_stack subfields. Other constraints stay free-form via additionalProperties.

tech_stack.description: v0.6 additive — structured runtime / language / test / build declarations. Consumed by the gate runner's pre-flight check.

build.description: none | vite | webpack | rollup | esbuild | setuptools | ...

decisions.description: v0.6 additive — Architecture Decision Records. The product-planner writes them into plan.md, Mode B-2 converts them here, and render_domain.py renders them into domain.md's ## Decisions section.

context.description: Why this decision was needed — the background

consequences.description: Positive and negative outcomes of the decision

tags.description: Open string: security · auth · ui · brand · stack · perf · data · api · a11y · ci · deploy · etc.

supersedes.description: The ids of earlier ADRs this one replaces. The replaced ADRs auto-transition to status=superseded.

superseded_by.description: The id of the ADR that replaced this one (when present). Mode B-2 fills it in as the inverse of supersedes.

risks.description: v0.6 additive — risk catalog. The product-planner writes it into plan.md and Mode B-2 converts. Direct input for qa-engineer's risk-based testing.

id.description: v0.10.7 (returned via cosmic-suika I-004): both R-N and RISK-N are accepted. The RISK- prefix was added for consistency with the other id conventions (BR-N · ADR-N · F-N · AC-N) — additive backward-compat, so existing R-N entries keep working.

metadata.description: v2.3.8 promotion — the eight blocks are now first-class metadata.* fields. metadata.extensions.* is accepted for backward compatibility (deprecated).

source.description: Conversion provenance. Common shape: {origin, maturity, revision}. F-036 added 'existing_code' as an expected origin value (brownfield repo seed via /harness-boot:init option 3). Existing values 'idea' / 'planning_doc' / 'architecture_doc' / 'implementation' remain valid. additionalProperties: true is implicit — additive only.

host_subtype.description: Subtype discriminator from meta adapter v0.2 §3.2 (intended for the meta domain initially — other domains use 'mixed' or omit).

style_fingerprint.description: F-037 (Layer B fog-clear) — project-level style cache. Populated deterministically by scripts/scan/style_fingerprint.fingerprint() during work.activate. Read by scripts/ceremonies/kickoff.py to inject style context into the implementer prompt. All sub-fields optional and additive.

area_index.description: F-037 — registry of areas the fog-clear hook has reconnoitered. Canonical persistence is the .harness/area_index.yaml side file; this schema field is reserved for users who choose to inline the registry into spec.yaml. Each entry: slug, label, modules, paths, chapter_path, last_scanned_ts, first_seen_feature_id, _provenance.

fog.description: F-037 — opt-out + bound knobs for fog-clear. disabled=true skips the autowire entirely. max_chapters caps the .harness/chapters/ growth (default 32). max_paths_per_area caps how many paths each chapter lists (default 16).

extensions.$comment: v2.3.7 experimental namespace — deprecated in v2.3.8. The v2.3.8 loader promotes blocks found here into metadata.* and emits a warning. Slated for removal in v2.5.0.

agent_permissions.$comment: Slated for promotion in v2.4.0 — out of scope for v2.3.8. self v2 uses an object form ({matrix_source, schema, agents[]}); v2.4.0 will lift it into an array.

command_map.title: command_map[] — explicit function-signature declarations for entry_points
command_map.description: P0 MUST-if-present. 3/3 meta hits. Linked to features[].id via the handler reference.

id.description: Unique identifier — may equal the command string.

command.description: The command string registered with the host.

handler.description: Reference to features[].id (e.g. F-001) or any free-form feature identifier (e.g. F-001_init). /harness-boot:check verifies whether it matches an existing features[].id.

kind.description: Permitted values vary by subtype: self-bootstrap (slash-command / build-step / contract-schema), build-tool (vite-subcommand / rollup-plugin / plugin-factory / webpack-plugin), editor-extension (vscode-command / jetbrains-action / sublime-command). Optional — when set, /harness-boot:check enforces the per-subtype values.

command_map.$comment: Duplicate ids are forbidden (programmatic check) — JSONSchema cannot express it. self-bootstrap connects to a feature via preamble_required / gate / requires_state instead of kind — kind is optional.

ambient_files.title: ambient_files[] — workspace files the spec creates or reads
ambient_files.description: P0 MUST-if-present. 3/3 meta hits. Globs are only allowed when required=false.

kind.$comment: Field-tested recommended values: ssot / cache / expanded_cache / hash_cache / index_cache / host_config / draft_buffer / lockfile / artifact / log / append_only_log / report / state / shim / agent_definitions / skill_definitions / hook_definitions / other. Diverse across subtypes — kept open-string.

host_binding.title: host_binding[] — version contract with the host program
host_binding.description: P0 SHOULD (conditional MUST for host-plugin subtypes). 2/2 host-plugin hits. N/A for self-bootstrap.

host.description: vite / vscode / rollup / esbuild / webpack / jetbrains / cursor / vscodium / ...

drift_catalog.title: drift_catalog[] — expected kinds of spec / reality drift and how to handle them

versioning_axes.title: versioning_axes[] — independently versioned axes
versioning_axes.description: P0 MUST-if-present. 3/3 meta hits. Each axis carries its own semver and policy.

policy.$comment: Recommended values: semver_strict / semver_with_migration / semver_with_regenerate / semver_with_pre / stable_contract / append_only_events_log / calver / custom. Diverse by domain — kept open-string.

contribution_points.title: contribution_points[] — UI / behavior surfaces contributed to the host
contribution_points.description: P1 SHOULD. 3/3 meta hits. The accepted `kind` set differs by subtype — see meta adapter v0.2 §4.8 host matrix.

kind.$comment: Per-subtype allowed values (meta adapter v0.2 §4.8): self-bootstrap = buildStep / contractSchema / slashCommand; build-tool = pluginHook / cliSubcommand / configSchema; editor-extension = command / keybinding / view / configuration / menus / statusbar / activationEvents / webview / language / debugger. Not closed by enum — kept open-string (subtype validation happens in /harness-boot:check).

preamble_contract.title: preamble_contract — contract for the text emitted at process start
preamble_contract.description: P1 MAY. 3/3 meta hits. required=true is typical for self-bootstrap; false is typical for host-plugins.

format.$comment: self v2 uses '3-line' (a 3-row banner with line_1/2/3). Treat it as a special case of multi-line-banner — v2.3.9 may consolidate the format taxonomy.

changelog.title: changelog[] — spec-level change history
changelog.description: P2 MAY. Consistent with P-24. 3/3 meta hits.

items.$comment: v0.10.7 (returned via cosmic-suika I-007): version is optional. Entries with only date · note are accepted — avoids forcing placeholder values.

gate_chain.title: gate_chain[] — CI/CD or runtime gate chain
gate_chain.description: P1 MAY (conditional). 1/3 full (self-bootstrap), 2/3 partial (host-plugin folds it into drift_catalog.action). Two styles are accepted: (A) on_fail / blocks, (B) prerequisite / blocking — self v2 uses style B.

prerequisite.description: Style B — list of gate ids that must be satisfied before this gate runs.

blocking.description: Style B — when true, a failure halts subsequent gates (equivalent to on_fail=stop).
